humanities_role_explanation: Uses artworks from 8 cultural traditions as visual content AND evaluates cultural understanding capabilities through art-historical interpretation across 5 hierarchical levels (L1-L5)
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Vision-language evaluation combining artwork images with expert-written bilingual critiques. Models generate art criticism text based on visual analysis of cultural artworks.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Five-layer framework (L1-L5) from visual perception to philosophical aesthetics. Tasks require progressively deeper cultural knowledge: visual recognition (L1), technical analysis (L2), cultural symbolism (L3), historical context (L4), and philosophical aesthetics (L5). Evaluated through dimension coverage of culture-specific concepts.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Comprehensive multicultural VLM benchmark for cultural understanding evaluation across 8 traditions

evaluates_generative_llm.note: GPT-4o, Claude-Sonnet-4.5, Gemini-2.5-Pro, Qwen3-VL-235B, GLM-4V-Flash evaluated in pilot experiments

llm_is_primary_system.note: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are the primary systems being benchmarked for cultural understanding capabilities

arts_humanities_data.note: Visual Arts and Performing Arts (8 cultural art traditions), History (historical context analysis), Philosophy (aesthetic theories), Religious Studies (Buddhist art, religious iconography)

quantitative_metrics.note: Dimension Coverage Rate (DCR), layer-wise performance gaps (ΔL), inter-annotator agreement (Cohen's κ), cultural-fact accuracy percentages

english_language.note: Paper in English with bilingual Chinese-English benchmark content

summary: VULCA-BENCH introduces a comprehensive multicultural art critique benchmark with 7,410 image-critique pairs across 8 cultural traditions, operationalized through a five-layer cultural understanding framework (L1-L5) with 225 culture-specific dimensions. The benchmark addresses gaps in existing VLM evaluation by measuring hierarchical cultural interpretation beyond surface-level visual perception.
key_findings: All evaluated VLMs show a consistent 25-40 percentage-point performance drop from visual perception (L1-L2) to cultural interpretation (L3-L5), with Gemini-2.5-Pro achieving highest DCR of 72.4% and GLM-4V-Flash lowest at 58.2%. Error analysis reveals systematic failures in surface-level terminology use, historical anachronism, and cultural conflation.
